# Fiagram Standalone – Copilot Instructions

## Commands

```bash
# Build
make build               # Build binary to build/standalone
make build-all           # Cross-compile for Linux/macOS/Windows (amd64 + arm64)

# Run
make run-server          # go run cmd/standalone/*.go

# Test
make test                # Run all tests
go test -v ./test/dao/database/... -run TestCreateAccount  # Single test by name
go test -v ./test/...    # All tests with verbose output

# Lint
make lint                # golangci-lint run ./...

# Code generation
make generate            # Regenerate internal/generated/openapi/oapi.gen.go from docs/openapi.yml

# Dependencies
make tidy                # go mod tidy
make vendor              # go mod vendor

# Database migrations
make migrate-up-dev      # Apply pending migrations
make migrate-down-dev    # Rollback last migration
make migrate-status      # Show current migration state
make migrate-new <name>  # Create new migration file
```

## Architecture

The request flow follows a strict layered dependency chain:

```
cmd/standalone/main.go          ← Cobra CLI entrypoint
  └─ internal/app/standalone.go ← Uber fx module composition
       ├─ configs/               ← YAML config loading (embedded default or file path)
       ├─ logger/                ← Zap logger
       ├─ dao/
       │    ├─ database/         ← SQL queries against MySQL + migrations
       │    └─ cache/            ← Redis/in-memory caching (tokens, username dedup)
       ├─ logic/
       │    ├─ account/          ← Account CRUD business logic
       │    ├─ token/            ← JWT generation and validation
       │    └─ http/             ← HTTP request handlers (implement generated interfaces)
       └─ handler/
            ├─ http/             ← Gin router, middleware (CORS, JWT verification)
            └─ chatbot/          ← Webhook server
```

**Generated code:** `internal/generated/openapi/oapi.gen.go` is auto-generated by `oapi-codegen` from `docs/openapi.yml`. Do not edit it directly — run `make generate` after changing the spec.

## Key Conventions

### Uber fx Dependency Injection
Every layer exposes a `Module` variable:
```go
var Module = fx.Module("layer-name",
    fx.Provide(NewConstructor),
    fx.Invoke(StartHook),
)
```
Constructors follow `NewXxx(deps...) (Type, error)`. Types are matched by Go type, not by name. Use `fx.In` structs for multiple dependencies.

### DAO Accessor Pattern
All DAO types implement a `WithExecutor` method to support transactions:
```go
type AccountAccessor interface {
    CreateAccount(ctx context.Context, account Account) (id uint64, err error)
    WithExecutor(exec Executor) AccountAccessor
}

// In logic, for transactional operations:
tx, _ := db.BeginTx(ctx, nil)
defer tx.Rollback()
id, err := a.accountAccessor.WithExecutor(tx).CreateAccount(ctx, account)
tx.Commit()
```
Use `?` placeholders for all MySQL queries.

### HTTP Handler Pattern
Handlers are methods on logic structs that satisfy the oapi-codegen-generated `StrictServerInterface`. Route groups:
- `public` — no auth required (`/auth/signup`, `/auth/signin`, `/auth/token/refresh`)
- `authorized` — requires `verifyAccessToken` middleware (`/profile/*`)

The JWT middleware extracts `accountId` from the Bearer token and stores it in `gin.Context`.

### Error Handling
```go
// Wrap errors with context
return fmt.Errorf("failed to create account: %w", err)

// Predefined sentinel errors for expected failure cases
var ErrUsernameTaken = errors.New("username already taken")

// HTTP responses use typed schemas from the generated OpenAPI types:
// BadRequest, Unauthorized, InternalServerError (defined in docs/openapi.yml)
```

### Testing
- Tests live in `test/`, mirroring the `internal/` structure.
- Each test package has a `main_test.go` with `TestMain` that sets up real infrastructure (MySQL via Docker, migrations applied automatically).
- Use `github.com/stretchr/testify/require` (not `assert`) for all assertions.
- Config for tests: `configs.NewConfig("")` loads the embedded default (`configs/local.yaml`).

### Configuration
```go
config, err := configs.NewConfig("")           // Embedded default (configs/local.yaml)
config, err := configs.NewConfig("./my.yaml")  // External file
```
Key config fields: `Http`, `Auth` (JWT secrets, TTLs, bcrypt cost), `DatabaseClient`, `CacheClient`, `Log`.

### Naming
- Packages: lowercase, single word (`handler`, `logic`, `dao`)
- Sub-packages: domain name (`account`, `token`, `cache`, `database`)
- Interfaces: descriptive noun, often suffixed with `Accessor` for DAOs or `Logic` for business logic
- All exported symbols: PascalCase; unexported: camelCase

## Feature Development Workflow

Follow these steps in order when implementing a new feature.

### 1. Understand the Codebase
Before touching any code, read the relevant parts of the codebase:
- Trace the layer chain affected by the feature: which DAO accessors, logic structs, and handlers are involved.
- Read the OpenAPI spec (`docs/openapi.yml`) to understand existing API contracts.
- Check `internal/generated/openapi/oapi.gen.go` to understand what interfaces the feature must satisfy.

### 2. Plan
Clarify and document before writing code:
- **Requirements** — Confirm what the feature must do and any edge cases (auth rules, validation, error cases).
- **Naming** — Decide names for new types, methods, packages, DB columns, and OpenAPI `operationId`s following the conventions in this file.
- **Scope** — Identify every file that needs to change across all layers (openapi spec → generated code → DAO → logic → handler → migration if needed).

### 3. Generate a Task Checklist
Break the work into ordered, concrete tasks before coding. A typical feature checklist looks like:
- [ ] Update `docs/openapi.yml` with new paths, schemas, and responses
- [ ] Run `make generate` to regenerate `internal/generated/openapi/oapi.gen.go`
- [ ] Add migration file (`make migrate-new <name>`) and write the DDL
- [ ] Implement DAO accessor methods (interface + implementation + `WithExecutor`)
- [ ] Wire new DAO into `internal/dao/module.go`
- [ ] Implement logic layer (business logic, call DAO)
- [ ] Wire new logic into `internal/logic/module.go`
- [ ] Implement HTTP handler methods to satisfy the generated interface
- [ ] Register new routes in `internal/handler/http/server.go`
- [ ] Write tests (see step 5)

### 4. Code
Implement tasks in checklist order — spec and migrations first, then DAO, then logic, then handler. This ensures each layer has a stable contract before the layer above it is written.

### 5. Write Tests
Write tests based on the feature requirements, not the implementation:
- One `main_test.go` per test package with `TestMain` for infrastructure setup.
- Cover the happy path and every error/edge case from the requirements.
- Use `require` (not `assert`) for all assertions.
- Test file location mirrors the package being tested under `test/`.

### 6. Run Tests and Review on Failure
```bash
make test                                            # Full suite
go test -v ./test/<layer>/... -run <TestName>        # Single test
```
If any test fails:
1. Re-read the failing test's assertion against the original requirements.
2. Determine whether the bug is in the **implementation** or a **misunderstanding of requirements**.
3. If requirements were misunderstood, go back to **step 2 (Plan)** and revise before changing code.
4. If it is an implementation bug, fix it and re-run.

### 7. Iterate Until All Tests Pass
Repeat step 6 until `make test` exits cleanly. Do not mark a feature done until the full test suite passes.

---

## Tech Stack

| Concern | Library |
|---|---|
| HTTP | `github.com/gin-gonic/gin` |
| Dependency Injection | `go.uber.org/fx` |
| Logging | `go.uber.org/zap` |
| JWT | `github.com/golang-jwt/jwt/v5` |
| MySQL driver | `github.com/go-sql-driver/mysql` |
| Redis | `github.com/redis/go-redis/v9` |
| DB migrations | `github.com/rubenv/sql-migrate` |
| OpenAPI codegen | `github.com/oapi-codegen/oapi-codegen/v2` |
| CLI | `github.com/spf13/cobra` |
| Testing | `github.com/stretchr/testify/require` |
